steps.title: Isolate the term containing k
steps.content: Start with $17j + 4k = m$. Subtract $17j$ from both sides: $4k = m - 17j$.

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 4: $k = \dfrac{m - 17j}{4}$.
  • A ($k = \frac{m - 17j}{4}$): Matches exactly. That matches Choice A — $k=\frac{m-17j}{4}$.
  • B ($k = \frac{1}{4}m - 17j$): Only $m$ is divided by 4; $17j$ should also be divided by 4 — wrong.
  • C ($k = \frac{17j - m}{4}$): Numerator is negated; subtracting $17j$ gives $m - 17j$, not $17j - m$ — wrong sign.
  • D ($k = 4m - 17j$): Multiplies $m$ by 4 instead of dividing — wrong operation.
